title: pellaea ambigua
intro: Pellaea ambigua, also known as the umbrella fern, is a species of fern in the family Pteridaceae, characterized by its distinctive umbrella-like fronds. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. Pellaea ambigua has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared as an infusion or tincture, with herbal preparations often involving the use of the plant's leaves and roots in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
